vllm.title: [Kernel] `w4a16` support for `compressed-tensors`
vllm.author: dsikka
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Adds an additional CompressedTensorsScheme to supoprt w4a16 models, CompressedTensorsW4A16
Add support for w4a16 models quantized through sparseml and saved through compressed-tensors
Leverages the kernels (gptq_marlin_gemm) used for gptq_marlin

From Neural Magic, Co-authored by @alexm-neu

vllm.title: [CI/Build][REDO] Add is_quant_method_supported to control quantization test configurations
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
There were many separate uses of checking CUDA compute capability in order to run quantization tests, so it is best practice to have one function to be the source-of-truth to check support.
Instead of each quantization test having duplicated code such as:
from vllm.model_executor.layers.quantization

vllm.title: [Hardware][Intel] Optimize CPU backend and add more performance tips
vllm.author: bigPYJ1151
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR optimized CPU backend performance and added more performance tips.

Optimized input shape of torch_sdpa to use fast code path for better TTFT (~40% reduction).
Added tip and example to use TCMalloc, it will significantly improve the performance.
Initially integrated Paged attention from Inte

vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds a function to vllm.utils to get the current version of vLLM, removing some code duplication throughout the library.
Related contributions
This is part 3/5 of #5004.

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Simplify OpenAI server setup in tests
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR moves the majority of setup logic for ServerRunner inside the common test utilities, and renames ServerRunner to RemoteOpenAIServer.
Also, we no longer use the default port of 8000, instead using vllm.utils.get_open_port to automatically select an available one. This can avoid conflicts when

vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Improve the docs for LLaVA-1.5 and LLaVA-NeXT.

vllm.title: [Kernel] Factor out epilogues from cutlass kernels
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This is a straight refactor to the cutlass kernels, to separate the epilogues out of the kernel to allow adding new ones.
In particular, we're planning to define epilogues to handle cases of asymmetric quantization where fusion of the activation zero point terms is important for performance (see #39

vllm.author: comaniac
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Given that FP8 quantization is now achieving decent speedup, this PR removes its performance warning.

vllm.title: [Kernel] Disable CUTLASS kernels for fp8
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This fixes an illegal memory access during the execution of the fp8 CUTLASS kernels during the execution of a 20b granite model. At least until we know what's going on, let's revert back to scaled_mm.
To repro:
python3 -m "vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server" --model nm-testing/granite-20b-code-base-

vllm.title: Add `cuda_device_count_stateless`
vllm.author: Yard1
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Calling torch.cuda.device_count() before CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES is set to the desired value (during worker init) will lead to issues, as the env var will be read and persisted in memory, meaning later modifications will not affect it. This PR adds a way to obtain the device count without that issue.
F

vllm.title: [Hardware][Intel] Support CPU inference with AVX2 ISA
vllm.author: DamonFool
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Hi all,
Thanks to the work of #3634, vLLM can run on CPUs.
However, only AVX512 CPUs are supported at the moment.
So I'd like to support CPU inference with AVX2 CPUs.
This is because many machines are actually AVX2-only and not every developer has an AVX512 machine.
I believe vLLM would be more wide
